intro: Crossotropis grandiglumis, also known as African star grass, is a type of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and respiratory infections, due to its antimalarial, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Crossotropis grandiglumis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and is also used to make herbal extracts and tinctures for the treatment of various health issues.
